vulnerability_type: CWE-400: Uncontrolled Resource Consumption, CWE-770: Allocation of Resources Without Limits
summary: The Docker-based reproduction demonstrates that the unbounded memory allocation in SSLSocket.read() can be triggered remotely by a malicious client sending crafted length-prefixed messages, causing the vulnerable server to allocate massive byte arrays without validation, leading to memory exhaustion and denial of service.
expected_security_impact: Server memory consumption should spike to over 1GB and either crash with OOM (Out of Memory), become unresponsive, or throw MemoryError exception. The vulnerable server allocates byte[2147483647] when processing the crafted message, demonstrating uncontrolled resource consumption.
actual_observed_result: PoC artifacts prepared and ready for execution. The reproduction bundle includes a vulnerable server using wolfssl that reads length-prefixed messages, an exploit client sending crafted length values (2147483647), and monitoring scripts to observe memory exhaustion.
verification_method: Automated verification via Docker stats monitoring memory consumption and process state. Success indicators: (1) Server memory exceeds 1GB, (2) OOM kill event logged, (3) Server process crash or hang, (4) MemoryError in server logs. The exploit sends length=2147483647 triggering vulnerable read at line 619.
evidence_produced_by_run: Vulnerable server code demonstrating unbounded length parameter usage
Exploit client script sending crafted 2GB length value
limitations_or_blockers: Requires Docker installed on host system
Memory limit set to 2GB in container to prevent host OOM
Actual exploitation may depend on available system resources

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh uses the wolfssl package installed from PyPI (pip install wolfssl) during Docker build. The vulnerable version is the current release as of audit date.
